questions.question: What does `Box<T>` provide?
questions.explanation: Box<T> allocates data on the heap with a single owner. Useful for recursive types, large data, or trait objects.

questions.question: Rc<T> is safe to use across multiple threads.
questions.explanation: Rc<T> uses non-atomic reference counting and is NOT thread-safe. Use Arc<T> (atomic reference counting) for shared ownership across threads.

questions.question: Create a Box to enable this recursive type:
questions.explanation: Without Box, the recursive List type has infinite size. Box<List> gives it a known size (pointer) so the compiler accepts it.

questions.question: What is interior mutability in Rust?
options: A pattern that allows mutation through an immutable reference, with rules checked at runtime
questions.explanation: RefCell<T> provides interior mutability: you can get a mutable borrow from an immutable reference. Borrow rules are enforced at runtime instead of compile time.

questions.question: Share ownership between two variables using Rc:
questions.explanation: Rc::clone(&a) increments the reference count without deep-copying the data. Both a and b now share ownership of the String.

questions.question: The Deref trait lets smart pointers be treated like regular references with the `*` operator.
questions.explanation: Implementing Deref allows deref coercion: the compiler automatically converts &Box<T> to &T, making smart pointers transparent to use.
